title: Iran's neighbors, under drone attacks, confront their vulnerability
subhed: Lacking experience in countering kamikaze drones, especially the infamous Shahed models, the Gulf countries have shown how unprepared they were for the threat posed by Iran, which is determined to exploit their vulnerability.
byline: By Chloé Hoorman and Marie Jégo

title: US says it is nearing 'complete control' of Iran airspace
subhed: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the US and Israel 'will have complete control of Iranian skies, uncontested airspace.' Trump, meanwhile, rated the US performance in the war with Iran as a '15' on 'a scale of 10,' saying the Islamic Republic's leaders were rapidly being killed and that Tehran's arsenal of ballistic missiles was also being 'wiped out.'

title: Macron says he urged Netanyahu to 'refrain from a ground offensive' in Lebanon
subhed: The French president said he also spoke with Lebanon's president and prime minister, stressing the need for the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah to 'immediately cease its attacks against Israel and beyond.'  Meanwhile, the leader of Hezbollah vowed to keep up its fight against Israel, whose forces advanced in Lebanon for a third day.

title: For Lebanon's displaced, a bitter sense of déjà vu
subhed: The government reports that more than 58,000 people have fled their homes since the outbreak of the latest war between Israel and Hezbollah.

title: 'A war justified once again by a lie turns into a race against the clock for Donald Trump'
subhed: The third American war waged in the Middle East by a president of the United States bears little resemblance to the previous ones, observes Le Monde's columnist Gilles Paris.

What does international law say about the Israeli-American attack on Iran and Tehran's retaliation?
subhed: Aggression, self-defense, preemptive strikes: The United States, Israel and Iran each justified their military actions, but legal experts question their legality.

title: Iranian regime scrambles to survive after Khamenei's death
subhed: Amid scenes of celebration and mourning, the formation of an interim government and ongoing bombings, Iran has entered a period of profound uncertainty, where the Islamic Republic's very survival and the reshaping of its internal balance of power are at stake.

title: France's Iranian artistic diaspora torn between anxiety and joy
subhed: Since the US-Israeli strikes on Iran, artists in the diaspora have found themselves dreaming of better days, even as they fear a rising death toll.
